faqs.a: Racing thoughts are fast, continuous streams of thinking that move quicker than a person can sort or act on them, jumping from one topic to the next. They can be worried, excited, or just relentless, and they're hard to slow down on purpose.

faqs.q: What conditions are racing thoughts linked to?
faqs.a: They show up across several conditions, including anxiety, the mania or hypomania of bipolar disorder, and during acute stress or sleep deprivation. The surrounding context helps a clinician understand what they're pointing to.

faqs.q: Why do racing thoughts often interfere with sleep?
faqs.a: Lying in bed, the thoughts can pick up speed instead of settling, which is one reason racing thoughts and trouble sleeping so often travel together. The speed itself can make it hard to feel rested.
